title: Onboarding Snapshot: Compact Hosted API Quicklaunch Canvas
preview: Create a compact, single-screen focused suite of getting-started screenshots and micro-animations for the Hosted API quickstart. The goal is a "mini-canvas" that combines: model selection, a lightweight request-builder ("Mini-Composer"), a live code snippet panel (JS / Python / cURL) and a streaming response preview...
text: Create a compact, single-screen focused suite of getting-started screenshots and micro-animations for the Hosted API quickstart. The goal is a "mini-canvas" that combines: model selection, a lightweight request-builder ("Mini-Composer"), a live code snippet panel (JS / Python / cURL) and a streaming response preview that highlights the EventSource/streaming integration. Visuals should support both light and dark modes and include small callouts for key concepts: model affinity badges, inferred token cost, and whether prefix/KV cache is being used. These assets will be used in docs, the quickstart modal inside the Console, and marketing one-pagers for the March release.

title: Compact viewport sampler and ARIA summaries for embedded time-series
preview: Problem: Compact time-series thumbnails (list/table rows, sidecar cards) are rendering full-resolution paths and verbose tooltips which increases CPU work, memory churn, and creates noisy screen reader output when many charts are present. We need a design solution that reduces render cost, keeps visual fidelity for ...
text: Problem: Compact time-series thumbnails (list/table rows, sidecar cards) are rendering full-resolution paths and verbose tooltips which increases CPU work, memory churn, and creates noisy screen reader output when many charts are present. We need a design solution that reduces render cost, keeps visual fidelity for trend/peak detection, and produces concise, stable ARIA-friendly summaries that don't flood assistive technology.

title: Dark-mode and accessibility pass for onboarding assets and quickstart visuals
preview: Perform a focused accessibility and dark-mode review for all onboarding / quickstart UX assets (illustrations, iconography, screenshots, and docs visuals) used in the public quickstart flow and internal 'first-run' product tour. Goal: meet WCAG AA contrast for text and interactive elements, ensure imagery and icons ...
text: Perform a focused accessibility and dark-mode review for all onboarding / quickstart UX assets (illustrations, iconography, screenshots, and docs visuals) used in the public quickstart flow and internal 'first-run' product tour. Goal: meet WCAG AA contrast for text and interactive elements, ensure imagery and icons are readable in dark contexts, provide alternate assets and implementation guidance so engineering can swap assets without visual regressions.

title: Caret stability for tokenized contenteditable inputs: preserve selection and reduce jumpiness
preview: Problem: our tokenized multi-value input (used for recipients, tags, and rule-expressions) relies on contenteditable wrappers for rich behavior. On a set of platforms and edge cases (mobile IME, long paste, browser autofill, and when tokens are recycled during reflow) the caret jumps to the end or selection collapse...
text:
Problem: our tokenized multi-value input (used for recipients, tags, and rule-expressions) relies on contenteditable wrappers for rich behavior. On a set of platforms and edge cases (mobile IME, long paste, browser autofill, and when tokens are recycled during reflow) the caret jumps to the end or selection collapses unexpectedly. This causes frequent user frustration and produces lost input when users are typing quickly or accepting suggested completions.

Goal: define a stable, cross-platform caret-preservation pattern and interaction heuristics for our contenteditable-backed token inputs. Produce design guidance, a small interaction spec for engineering, and an acceptance checklist that prevents regressions. Include fallbacks for browsers where precise caret restores are unreliable and ensure ARIA behavior for screen readers remains correct.

title: Harmonize input composition: placeholders, autocompletion, and screen reader semantics
preview: Audit and standardize interaction behaviors across text inputs, selects, and toggles with a focus on placeholders, browser/autocomplete hints, and screen reader semantics. The goal is consistent UX and improved accessibility across platforms and browsers while aligning with tokenized spacing and color roles.
text: Audit and standardize interaction behaviors across text inputs, selects, and toggles with a focus on placeholders, browser/autocomplete hints, and screen reader semantics. The goal is consistent UX and improved accessibility across platforms and browsers while aligning with tokenized spacing and color roles.

title: Guided observability starter frames — mini tutorial sequence for latency, cost-per-token, and trace views
preview: Design a compact, guided screenshot sequence and annotated assets for the Observability quickstart that walks new users through three micro-scenarios: per-route latency analysis, cost-per-token breakdown, and an end-to-end trace waterfall. Deliverables include layered Figma frames for web and compact mobile, annotat...
text: Design a compact, guided screenshot sequence and annotated assets for the Observability quickstart that walks new users through three micro-scenarios: per-route latency analysis, cost-per-token breakdown, and an end-to-end trace waterfall. Deliverables include layered Figma frames for web and compact mobile, annotated copy for in-product overlays, export-ready PNG/JPEG assets for docs and release notes, and a short micro-tutorial script for the product tour team.

title: Release-note screenshot pack for model launches and retirements
preview: Goal: Produce a polished, platform-consistent screenshot and visual asset pack used in release notes, quickstart pages, and the model catalog UI to announce new model additions and planned deprecations. Scope includes: (1) hero screenshot for release notes (web and mobile widths), (2) model detail tile mockups showi...
text: Goal: Produce a polished, platform-consistent screenshot and visual asset pack used in release notes, quickstart pages, and the model catalog UI to announce new model additions and planned deprecations. Scope includes: (1) hero screenshot for release notes (web and mobile widths), (2) model detail tile mockups showing performance metrics and pricing, (3) deprecation banners and migration CTA visuals, (4) small inline thumbnails for changelog lists, and (5) a short guide for engineers/content authors on which image to use where and filename conventions. Deliver assets in PNG and SVG, include a Figma file with labeled components and export slices. Follow the Redwood visual system (colors, type scale) and make sure pricing breakdowns match the latest pricing spec from PM.

title: Python SDK: event-bridge streaming chat completion ergonomics and retry contract
preview:
Goal:
Improve the Python SDK developer ergonomics for streaming chat completions by introducing an 'event-bridge' abstraction that cleanly surfaces both event-mode and incremental token streams, plus a clearly specified retry/resume contract for transient network and server errors.

Motivation:
Current feedback show...
text:
Goal:
Improve the Python SDK developer ergonomics for streaming chat completions by introducing an 'event-bridge' abstraction that cleanly surfaces both event-mode and incremental token streams, plus a clearly specified retry/resume contract for transient network and server errors.

Motivation:
Current feedback shows users repeatedly implementing ad-hoc adapters to convert event payloads into higher-level chat messages (for UI) or to reconcile partial tokens after retries. We want a single, well-documented primitive that: 1) provides an async iterator over high-level 'StreamEvent' objects, 2) exposes a low-level raw event stream when needed, 3) carries resume/checkpoint tokens for safe retries, and 4) integrates backpressure semantics compatible with asyncio applications.

Success criteria / Acceptance:
- New API: SDK exposes Chat.stream_events(...) that yields StreamEvent objects wit...

title: Stream pipeline: context propagation and early termination ergonomics for Go SDK
preview: Problem statement: The current Go SDK stream surface does not make it easy for callers to reliably abort in-flight streams, propagate deadlines to internal goroutines, or observe partial results when an operation is terminated early. This has resulted in customer reports of leaked goroutines during canceled requests...
text:
Problem statement: The current Go SDK stream surface does not make it easy for callers to reliably abort in-flight streams, propagate deadlines to internal goroutines, or observe partial results when an operation is terminated early. This has resulted in customer reports of leaked goroutines during canceled requests, unclear semantics when context deadlines elapse during streaming, and poor ergonomics for common patterns (timeout per-chunk vs overall deadline).

Goal: Rework the streaming implementation and public ergonomics to provide a predictable pipeline model that: 1) reliably propagates cancellation and deadlines to all internal workers; 2) supports early termination with deterministic cleanup; 3) exposes a small set of helpers/examples for common timeouts (per-chunk, overall, and graceful drain); and 4) preserves/respects retry semantics for transient connection errors without ...

title: Raftless throttler drain + sticky fairness protocol for limiter outage recovery
preview: Implement a lightweight, non-consensus (raftless) drain and recovery mode for token/lease-based throttlers to maintain sticky fairness during limiter outages and cross-region failovers. Goal: ensure bounded customer impact during limiter restarts, network partitions, and regional circuit-breaker activations while ke...
text: Implement a lightweight, non-consensus (raftless) drain and recovery mode for token/lease-based throttlers to maintain sticky fairness during limiter outages and cross-region failovers. Goal: ensure bounded customer impact during limiter restarts, network partitions, and regional circuit-breaker activations while keeping 99th percentile latency and error SLOs intact for premium tenants.

title: Go SDK model-mapper, validators and streaming facade
preview: Goal: Add a minimal, ergonomic layer to the Go SDK that 1) maps raw generation envelopes into strongly-typed model structs using a small set of generics helpers, 2) validates structured output contracts at decode time with pluggable validation rules, and 3) provides a streaming facade (async iterator-like) that expo...
text: Goal: Add a minimal, ergonomic layer to the Go SDK that 1) maps raw generation envelopes into strongly-typed model structs using a small set of generics helpers, 2) validates structured output contracts at decode time with pluggable validation rules, and 3) provides a streaming facade (async iterator-like) that exposes decoded items and surface-level backpressure controls for callers. This ticket focuses on API shape, reference implementation, and examples (not final perf tuning).

title: Tenant-aware observability pipeline: deterministic pseudonymization, retention enforcer, and access audit
preview: Build a tenant-aware observability ingestion and downstream pipeline that removes or pseudonymizes tenant-sensitive fields deterministically, enforces retention at the dataset/tenant level, and produces cryptographic access audit trails. This ticket scopes the core pipeline and the integration points needed for trac...
text: Build a tenant-aware observability ingestion and downstream pipeline that removes or pseudonymizes tenant-sensitive fields deterministically, enforces retention at the dataset/tenant level, and produces cryptographic access audit trails. This ticket scopes the core pipeline and the integration points needed for tracing hooks, metric/log separation, and the dashboard-level access audit views. The goal is to allow product teams to continue troubleshooting with meaningful telemetry while ensuring PII is protected and access to raw identifiers is auditable and controllable.

title: Trace Ingest Translator: infer-schema and enrich spans for mixed OTLP/Zipkin clients
preview:
Background:
We ingest spans from a wide variety of clients: OTLP native SDKs, legacy Zipkin/Jaeger shims, and several internal SDK versions that emit nonstandard attributes. Downstream backends (Tempo, legacy Jaeger sinks) expect a relatively consistent schema for trace_id/span_id/timestamps and common attributes (s...
text:
Background:
We ingest spans from a wide variety of clients: OTLP native SDKs, legacy Zipkin/Jaeger shims, and several internal SDK versions that emit nonstandard attributes. Downstream backends (Tempo, legacy Jaeger sinks) expect a relatively consistent schema for trace_id/span_id/timestamps and common attributes (service.name, http.method, db.statement, tenant).

Goal:
Build a lightweight Trace Ingest Translator component in the ingest path that: 1) infers and normalizes span schema from incoming envelopes, 2) applies deterministic attribute mapping and enrichment rules, 3) preserves tenant and sampling metadata, and 4) outputs a canonical OTLP-like envelope to Tempo and the Jaeger compatibility writer. The translator must be low-latency (target median added latency < 6ms per-span at p95 < 12ms under typical load) and horizontally sharding-friendly.

Scope / Non-goals:
- Include only...

title: Auth Observability After‑Action: RBACv2, SSO & Audit Pipeline SLOs/Alerts Remediation
preview: Summary: This ticket consolidates findings from the March 2026 auth/audit availability incident and defines a prioritized remediation plan across three areas: RBACv2 policy evaluation latency, SSO/SAML session handling under spike load, and the audit-event delivery pipeline. The goal is to make the auth surface obse...
text:
Summary: This ticket consolidates findings from the March 2026 auth/audit availability incident and defines a prioritized remediation plan across three areas: RBACv2 policy evaluation latency, SSO/SAML session handling under spike load, and the audit-event delivery pipeline. The goal is to make the auth surface observable and resilient with concrete SLOs and tuned alerting so regressions are caught earlier and with actionable signals.

What triggered this: A region-level capacity spike caused transient delays in token validation and SAML assertions leading to failed sign-ins and delayed audit-event ingestion. Multiple signals were noisy (high auth latency, partial 502s from the auth proxy, and audit sink lag), which slowed incident diagnosis.

High-level proposed outcomes: 1) Define and publish auth SLOs and error budgets; 2) Reduce blind spots by adding end-to-end observability for R...

title: Adaptive onboarding checklist and pinning policy for experimental quantized models
preview: Establish an adaptive metadata schema, onboarding checklist, and a conservative pinning policy for newly-ingested experimental quantized variants (Q4/Q8/Q5) so that performance profiles are published reliably and runtime fallbacks behave predictably. This ticket covers schema additions, validation tooling, documenta...
text: Establish an adaptive metadata schema, onboarding checklist, and a conservative pinning policy for newly-ingested experimental quantized variants (Q4/Q8/Q5) so that performance profiles are published reliably and runtime fallbacks behave predictably. This ticket covers schema additions, validation tooling, documentation updates for the catalog, and a short-term rollout policy that balances dev velocity with safety for production customers.

title: Reusable spark-band component with ARIA summary and lazy aggregation
preview:
Problem: Several dashboards rely on bespoke sparkline and band components implemented inconsistently across products. This leads to duplicated logic, accessibility variance (different aria summaries), and performance regressions when many small charts render on the same page.

Goal: Design a single, reusable 'spark-...
text:
Problem: Several dashboards rely on bespoke sparkline and band components implemented inconsistently across products. This leads to duplicated logic, accessibility variance (different aria summaries), and performance regressions when many small charts render on the same page.

Goal: Design a single, reusable 'spark-band' primitive optimized for dense time-series displays. It should: (1) provide a concise ARIA summary for screen readers, (2) expose a small API for visual and interaction variants (line, area, band, micro-bar), (3) support lazy aggregation/decimation for high-point series, (4) adopt tokens for color/spacing, and (5) include a Canvas-first implementation with an accessible DOM fallback for legacy browsers/screen readers.

title: Consolidate surface tokens and compact variants for dense dataviz kit
preview: Problem: The DataViz surface kit (buttons, table rows, chart legends, small density cards) has grown a high-entropy set of visual-only variants and duplicated surface tokens leading to publish failures, inconsistent runtime spacing, and bloated instance counts. Goal: Reduce variant entropy by collapsing visually red...
text: Problem: The DataViz surface kit (buttons, table rows, chart legends, small density cards) has grown a high-entropy set of visual-only variants and duplicated surface tokens leading to publish failures, inconsistent runtime spacing, and bloated instance counts. Goal: Reduce variant entropy by collapsing visually redundant variants into token-driven states, normalize token names to the code spec, and add a non-destructive publish pipeline with preflight checks and a targeted instance migration plan. This work should enable predictable library publishes, make auto-layout constraints resilient for responsive breakpoints, and reduce downstream engineering churn when mapping Figma tokens to CSS/Design tokens. Deliverables: 1) Audit spreadsheet of all surface tokens and variants across DataViz libraries. 2) Variant compression plan that documents which visual differences become tokens vs wh...

title: Prefill Presets Canvas & Risk-Aware Configuration Flow
preview: Design a compact, canvas-style configuration experience that lets engineers pick, preview, and tune prefill and batching presets with embedded risk-aware guardrails and inline explainers. This flows aims to reduce cognitive load when teams adopt batching/caching optimizations by surfacing trade-offs (latency vs cost...
text: Design a compact, canvas-style configuration experience that lets engineers pick, preview, and tune prefill and batching presets with embedded risk-aware guardrails and inline explainers. This flows aims to reduce cognitive load when teams adopt batching/caching optimizations by surfacing trade-offs (latency vs cost vs reliability), simulating effects on a representative workload, and providing safe defaults and rollback guidance for production rollouts.

title: Tiered fidelity, spoken briefs, and event coalescing for timeline/series charts
preview: Goal: Define a design spec and interaction pattern for time-series timeline charts that reduces CPU/GPU work under constrained conditions while preserving a useful, accessible summary for keyboard and screen-reader users. This ticket covers three coordinated pieces: 1) visual token migration to support fidelity prof...
text:
Goal: Define a design spec and interaction pattern for time-series timeline charts that reduces CPU/GPU work under constrained conditions while preserving a useful, accessible summary for keyboard and screen-reader users. This ticket covers three coordinated pieces: 1) visual token migration to support fidelity profiles, 2) a spoken-briefs accessibility mode (concise TTS summaries on focus/hovers), and 3) event coalescing rules to limit frequent re-rendering during high-frequency input (scrubbing/rapid live updates).

Why now: Observability dashboards and mobile customers report spikes in rendering cost and janky hovers when streams update at >10Hz or users scrub quickly. We need a predictable degradation strategy that product teams can opt into and designers and devs can implement consistently across chart components.

Scope: Design system tokens and component mixins for three fideli...

title: Adaptive surface layer and shadow geometry update
preview:
Problem: Our current token set treats elevation as a single numeric scale with static shadows and fixed surface colors. This leads to inconsistent perceived depth across components (tables, dialogs, cards) and creates friction when implementing dense vs. spacious product variants.

Goal: Produce an updated tokens sp...
text:
Problem: Our current token set treats elevation as a single numeric scale with static shadows and fixed surface colors. This leads to inconsistent perceived depth across components (tables, dialogs, cards) and creates friction when implementing dense vs. spacious product variants.

Goal: Produce an updated tokens spec that introduces: (1) adaptive surface layers that shift tone based on container context and elevation class; (2) shadow geometry tokens that describe directional light, umbra/penumbra scaling and micro-elevation offsets; (3) responsive radius presets tied to density breakpoints; (4) an aligned typography mapping for headline/body scales that better matches cap-height across sizes. The result should reduce per-component overrides, improve contrast in both light/dark modes, and make elevation feel consistent under variable densities.

Scope: tokens.json changes, component ...

title: Scalable patterns onboarding storyboard and screenshot suite
preview: Create a cohesive storyboard and screenshot suite that explains scalable capacity patterns for first-time Dedicated customers. This ticket covers: a 6-frame onboarding storyboard that visualizes capacity decisions (reserved vs burst), three responsive mockups (desktop, tablet, mobile) for the capacity setup flow, an...
text: Create a cohesive storyboard and screenshot suite that explains scalable capacity patterns for first-time Dedicated customers. This ticket covers: a 6-frame onboarding storyboard that visualizes capacity decisions (reserved vs burst), three responsive mockups (desktop, tablet, mobile) for the capacity setup flow, annotated screenshot variants for autoscaling rules and SLA overview, and a lightweight set of microcopy alternatives for error/fallback states. Deliverables should be ready for handoff to engineering and documentation teams and include assets optimized for the release notes and the in-app quickstart modal.

title: Button fallback story and soft-disabled state polish
preview: Polish and document a resilient fallback story for button tokens and introduce a 'soft-disabled' visual state for transient, cancelable actions. This ticket covers: token fallback rules when semantic tokens are missing or overridden, icon-only spacing regressions across themes, spinner overlap/escape hatch behavior,...
text: Polish and document a resilient fallback story for button tokens and introduce a 'soft-disabled' visual state for transient, cancelable actions. This ticket covers: token fallback rules when semantic tokens are missing or overridden, icon-only spacing regressions across themes, spinner overlap/escape hatch behavior, motion-reduction-safe micro-interactions, and final acceptance criteria for engineering handoff. Goal is to reduce visual regressions observed after token migration and make disabled feedback more informative without blocking cancelable flows.

title: Dynamic slew-rate limiter with progressive KV compaction to mitigate queue pileup at sustained high QPS
preview: Background: During recent weekend load tests and two customer incidents we observed sustained high QPS (50k+ req/s across the cluster) producing prolonged microbatch queue growth, increased tail latency, and KV cache pressure that eventually triggers out-of-memory (OOM) evictions and degraded throughput. Existing ad...
text:
Background: During recent weekend load tests and two customer incidents we observed sustained high QPS (50k+ req/s across the cluster) producing prolonged microbatch queue growth, increased tail latency, and KV cache pressure that eventually triggers out-of-memory (OOM) evictions and degraded throughput. Existing admission heuristics (static rate caps + simple headroom checks) react too late or oscillate under nonstationary traffic patterns.

Goal: Implement a coordinated admission and memory-reclamation strategy that prevents queue pileups before OOMs occur while preserving throughput and KV hit rate. Specifically: introduce a 'slew-rate limiter' to throttle the entrance of new microbatches smoothly and a progressive KV compaction policy to reclaim low-value cache entries when memory headroom falls below safe thresholds. The two mechanisms should be tenant-aware and integrate with ex...

title: Workload Signal Assembler & Continual Batching Advisor
preview: Build a recommendation engine that synthesizes workload signals (request size distribution, repeat prefixes, KV cache hit patterns, latency tail behavior) and outputs conservative continuous-batching parameters plus a safe apply/rollback plan. This ticket focuses on the signal assembler, heuristics for safe step siz...
text: Build a recommendation engine that synthesizes workload signals (request size distribution, repeat prefixes, KV cache hit patterns, latency tail behavior) and outputs conservative continuous-batching parameters plus a safe apply/rollback plan. This ticket focuses on the signal assembler, heuristics for safe step sizes, and the shadow-apply telemetry needed for automatic rollback triggers.

title: Output contract monitoring and rollout safety for shadow & canary traffic
preview:
Problem:
Teams running model rollouts need deterministic, low-friction checks to ensure new model variants do not break structured-output contracts or tool-calling behavior when traffic is gradually shifted (shadow, canary, A/B). Today we detect regressions late via downstream errors and manual rollbacks.

Goal:
- P...
text:
Problem:
Teams running model rollouts need deterministic, low-friction checks to ensure new model variants do not break structured-output contracts or tool-calling behavior when traffic is gradually shifted (shadow, canary, A/B). Today we detect regressions late via downstream errors and manual rollbacks.

Goal:
- Provide a runtime-compatible set of validators and diffing tools that run on shadowed and canary traffic to detect structured-output incompatibilities (broken schemas, missing required fields, unexpected types) and tool-call signature regressions (changed function names, param lists, arg types).
- Surface regression metrics in the Console and trigger automated rollback when contract-violation thresholds are exceeded, while minimizing false positives.

Proposed approach:
1) Contract Registry: keep a versioned, lightweight schema/contract for each route/structured-output and f...

title: KV key shaping heuristics and safe-config apply/rollback automation
preview: Objective: Build a repeatable system that recommends KV/prefix cache key shapes and TTL policies using hit-rate modeling and offline replay, and that can safely apply and rollback cache configuration changes in production. Scope includes: (1) offline hit-rate simulator and estimator for alternative keying strategies...
text: Objective: Build a repeatable system that recommends KV/prefix cache key shapes and TTL policies using hit-rate modeling and offline replay, and that can safely apply and rollback cache configuration changes in production. Scope includes: (1) offline hit-rate simulator and estimator for alternative keying strategies, (2) TTL policy optimizer that recommends tiered TTL buckets, and (3) an automated safe-apply workflow with canary rollout, real-time telemetry gates, and automatic rollback hooks. This ticket focuses on the engineering design, a reference implementation for a single critical route (chat.complete), and integration with the console rollout APIs.

title: Queue cohort optimizer with SLO mitigations and staged rollback
preview: Implement a cohort-based queue optimizer that recommends batching and caching configurations per traffic cohort, applies changes safely via staged shadow-apply and traffic-slicing, and provides automated rollback when latency/throughput SLOs degrade. This ticket covers the algorithm prototype, config application orc...
text: Implement a cohort-based queue optimizer that recommends batching and caching configurations per traffic cohort, applies changes safely via staged shadow-apply and traffic-slicing, and provides automated rollback when latency/throughput SLOs degrade. This ticket covers the algorithm prototype, config application orchestration, anomaly detection thresholds, and integration points for Console dashboards and rollout policies.

title: Model catalog: quantization decision templates and validation orchestrator
preview: Create a reusable set of per-model quantization decision templates (FP8 / INT8) and a validation orchestrator that runs calibration + accuracy checks before a quant profile becomes the model catalog default. This ticket defines the template schema, the calibration harness entrypoints, acceptance thresholds, CI gatin...
text: Create a reusable set of per-model quantization decision templates (FP8 / INT8) and a validation orchestrator that runs calibration + accuracy checks before a quant profile becomes the model catalog default. This ticket defines the template schema, the calibration harness entrypoints, acceptance thresholds, CI gating rules, and an automated rollback policy. Goal: reduce manual model onboarding work by 60% while keeping generation-quality regressions under defined limits.
links: gh-pr:968 - add quant template schema and parser

title: Guided POP routing with quantile-differential and link-safety score
preview: Problem statement: We need a region/POP (Point-of-Presence) assignment policy that is resilient to transient spikes that inflate p95 while preserving tail SLOs, and that can also rapidly avoid links showing degraded packet-loss or high retransmit rates. Existing approaches overreact to short-lived p95 excursions or ...
text:
Problem statement: We need a region/POP (Point-of-Presence) assignment policy that is resilient to transient spikes that inflate p95 while preserving tail SLOs, and that can also rapidly avoid links showing degraded packet-loss or high retransmit rates. Existing approaches overreact to short-lived p95 excursions or ignore cross-metric correlation.

Proposal summary: Implement a 'quantile-differential with link-safety' guider that computes a composite preference score per POP combining: (1) smoothed p50 and p95 (EWMA windowed), (2) the p95-p50 delta (as a sensitivity factor), and (3) a network link-safety penalty derived from loss/rtx/icmp-failure telemetry. The guider will provide ranked POP choices and a binary failover trigger when safety thresholds breach. It prioritizes stable low-latency POPs but gracefully shifts when tail-risk or network degradation is sustained.

Algorithm (hi...

title: Centralize GPU launch templates and autoscaler integration for Private VPC
preview: Background: Private deployments require a repeatable, auditable Terraform module that produces GPU-capable node groups for both ASG-based autoscaling and Karpenter-driven provisioning. Current infra scripts are duplicated across repos causing drift in launch template settings (IMDS, ENA drivers, GPU kernel modules, ...
text:
Background: Private deployments require a repeatable, auditable Terraform module that produces GPU-capable node groups for both ASG-based autoscaling and Karpenter-driven provisioning. Current infra scripts are duplicated across repos causing drift in launch template settings (IMDS, ENA drivers, GPU kernel modules, kubelet flags) and discrepant health-check behaviors between ASG and Karpenter nodes.

Goal: Create a single Terraform module (private_gpu_node_group) that centralizes launch template generation, user-data bootstrapping, AMI lookup, and autoscaler-compatible tags/labels so we can:
- Provision ASG-backed GPU node groups in VPC-private clusters with consistent health checks and lifecycle hooks.
- Provision Karpenter-compatible instance profiles and launch configuration artifacts so Karpenter can provision GPU nodes with identical bootstrapping.
- Enable seamless in-place upgr...

title: Python SDK: unified streaming sink + resume hooks for chat completions
preview: Problem summary: Users building streaming chat integrations with the Python SDK are facing three recurring pain points: 1) scattered callback shapes for incremental tokens vs structured events, 2) lack of a small, composable contract for checkpoint/resume semantics (needed for flaky networks and long-lived conversat...
text:
Problem summary: Users building streaming chat integrations with the Python SDK are facing three recurring pain points: 1) scattered callback shapes for incremental tokens vs structured events, 2) lack of a small, composable contract for checkpoint/resume semantics (needed for flaky networks and long-lived conversations), and 3) unclear retry/idempotency behavior when applying partial outputs to user state.

This ticket proposes a lightweight "unified streaming sink" API and resume hooks that: (a) normalize event shapes (start, delta, tool_call, structured_output, end, error, meta), (b) expose a checkpoint/resume contract with user-provided idempotency keys and explicit boundaries, and (c) specify retry semantics with guidance and helpers for common app patterns (UI streaming, transcript persistence, server-side reruns). The goal is ergonomic defaults plus explicit escape hatches for ...

title: Billing attestation: chain-of-custody, retention schedule, and least-privilege access matrix
preview: Create a repeatable attestation package for billing and metering evidence that satisfies SOC-style evidence requests and internal compliance checks. Deliverables include: a documented chain-of-custody for usage events, a retention schedule mapped to legal and commercial requirements, an explicit least-privilege acce...
text: Create a repeatable attestation package for billing and metering evidence that satisfies SOC-style evidence requests and internal compliance checks. Deliverables include: a documented chain-of-custody for usage events, a retention schedule mapped to legal and commercial requirements, an explicit least-privilege access matrix for services and people, and automated artifacts (signed snapshots, tamper-evident manifests) that can be exported to auditors.

title: Traffic fencing and model admission controller for heterogeneous environments
preview: Problem: We lack a single, lightweight mechanism to safely admit and fence model traffic across Hosted, Dedicated and Private installs. Existing rollout tooling assumes uniform networking and capacity semantics; Private/VPC and Dedicated clusters impose per-tenant quotas, stricter egress rules, and slower kernel upg...
text:
Problem: We lack a single, lightweight mechanism to safely admit and fence model traffic across Hosted, Dedicated and Private installs. Existing rollout tooling assumes uniform networking and capacity semantics; Private/VPC and Dedicated clusters impose per-tenant quotas, stricter egress rules, and slower kernel upgrade windows. That mismatch has caused noisy canaries and manual intervention during several recent model promotions.

Goal: Build a model admission controller + traffic fencing primitives that: 1) enforces per-environment constraints (capacity, network policy, KMS availability), 2) performs fast local rejection for incompatible requests, 3) integrates with canary/A-B/shadow flows to prevent noisy experiments from spilling into production, and 4) emits clear signals for automated rollback and human ops. The controller must be lightweight (config-first), pluggable into Redwo...

title: Stochastic arg-fuzz eval harness for function selection precision
preview: Goal: Build an automated, stochastic evaluation harness that detects regressions in function/tool selection accuracy and argument/schema validation after model, runtime, or config changes. This harness will exercise the tool-calling surface by generating randomized prompt variants, sampling model responses, performi...
text: Goal: Build an automated, stochastic evaluation harness that detects regressions in function/tool selection accuracy and argument/schema validation after model, runtime, or config changes. This harness will exercise the tool-calling surface by generating randomized prompt variants, sampling model responses, performing schema fuzzing on arguments, and measuring selection precision, false positive invocations, and argument conformance. The output should be a reproducible daily job, per-commit checks for PRs touching routing/config, and a dashboard with drift alerts and example failures for triage.

title: Routing decision audit trail and per-hop telemetry enhancements
preview: Scope: Add machine-readable decision logs and sampled distributed traces for the smart routing layer that performs region selection, tier routing, and fallback to model variants. Provide per-hop metrics (latency, queue depth, token count) emitted at each routing hop (edge proxy -> router -> model-host) and correlate...
text: Scope: Add machine-readable decision logs and sampled distributed traces for the smart routing layer that performs region selection, tier routing, and fallback to model variants. Provide per-hop metrics (latency, queue depth, token count) emitted at each routing hop (edge proxy -> router -> model-host) and correlate them by a trace_id and decision_id. Deliverables: 1) decision_log schema and storage plan (low-cost cold storage + short-term hot index), 2) cross-service trace propagation and instrumentation, 3) per-hop metrics with aggregation keys for route_policy, model_variant, region, tier, and customer_tier, 4) dashboards and alerting for anomalous routing patterns, and 5) sampling & redaction policy to maintain privacy and SLOs. Non-goal: full request payload retention; only metadata and short decision fingerprints retained per policy.

title: Inbound open-model risk bucketing and staged rollout contract
preview: Clarify requirements for how new open (community) model submissions are classified by risk, what metadata gates they must include, and the rollout contract that binds pinning, telemetry gating, and staged percentage exposure. This PRD fragment is intended to: 1) define discrete risk buckets (Low/Moderate/High) with ...
text: Clarify requirements for how new open (community) model submissions are classified by risk, what metadata gates they must include, and the rollout contract that binds pinning, telemetry gating, and staged percentage exposure. This PRD fragment is intended to: 1) define discrete risk buckets (Low/Moderate/High) with concrete signal thresholds; 2) specify minimum intake metadata required to accept an unpinned variant; 3) prescribe a staged rollout plan with telemetry gates and automatic rollback triggers; and 4) document operator responsibilities and handoffs to SRE/security for final admission. Goal is to give engineering clear pass/fail criteria for the intake pipeline and a deterministic rollout contract for Catalog and Runtime teams.

title: Client onramp prioritization rubric — language coverage, auth patterns & streaming ergonomics
preview: Problem: We receive a steady stream of customer requests for new language client libraries and auth/streaming ergonomics changes. Without a consistent intake and prioritization rubric we over-index on vocal requests and create engineering churn. This ticket defines a repeatable rubric and intake flow for triaging SD...
text:
Problem: We receive a steady stream of customer requests for new language client libraries and auth/streaming ergonomics changes. Without a consistent intake and prioritization rubric we over-index on vocal requests and create engineering churn. This ticket defines a repeatable rubric and intake flow for triaging SDK/client requests with a focus on enterprise needs (auth patterns, token rotation, SSO/OIDC, resumable streaming, backpressure) and language coverage trade-offs.

Goal: Establish a lightweight scoring rubric, an intake form, and a small gating checklist so PM/Eng/Design can rapidly decide: Build native SDK, provide official adapter, or document a community pattern. Deliverables:
- Prioritization rubric (weighted scores and thresholds)
- Intake form template for Solutions/Support/AE to capture signals
- Triage runbook with owners and SLAs (48h acknowledge, 10 business days i...

title: Quant presets and calibration playbook for model catalog defaults
preview: Create an operational playbook and implementation ticket set to choose, validate, and publish per-model quantization presets (FP8/INT8) in the model catalog with automated calibration runs and accuracy validation. The goal is to provide safe, documented defaults that ship with each catalog entry, plus automated guar...
text: Create an operational playbook and implementation ticket set to choose, validate, and publish per-model quantization presets (FP8/INT8) in the model catalog with automated calibration runs and accuracy validation. The goal is to provide safe, documented defaults that ship with each catalog entry, plus automated guardrails and rollback behavior so customers get predictable quality/cost trade-offs without manual experimentation.

title: Performance Studio: Per-Model and Per-Route Token Flow + Cost Layering Spec
preview: Goal: Define a compact, scannable ‘Performance Studio’ view that surfaces per-model and per-route token flow, latency distribution, throughput, and cost layers in a single composable canvas. Deliverables: 1) chart specs for Token Stream (waterfall + micro-lanes), Token Rate (sparklines + histogram), Latency Distribu...
text: Goal: Define a compact, scannable ‘Performance Studio’ view that surfaces per-model and per-route token flow, latency distribution, throughput, and cost layers in a single composable canvas. Deliverables: 1) chart specs for Token Stream (waterfall + micro-lanes), Token Rate (sparklines + histogram), Latency Distribution (p99/p95/p50 bands + kernel overlay), Throughput heatmap, and Cost Layering (stacked area that maps batching/quantization/cache savings to monetary units); 2) interaction patterns (time windowing, zoom, trace linkouts, model variant toggle); 3) accessibility and performance constraints; 4) annotated mockups and a Figma prototype for designer/PM/eng review. Scope: Per-route drilldown to show the top 5 model variants serving that route, token-level breakdown (prompt vs. generation vs. system tokens), and a cost attribution view that ties token segments to estimated cloud...

title: Traffic Mosaic: Guided Canary Composer with Contextual Rollback Hints
preview: Design and prototype a new 'Traffic Mosaic' composer in the Redwood Console that lets engineers visually construct gradual canary and A/B traffic plans, preview live signal risk, and surfaces contextual rollback hints tied to telemetry thresholds. The mosaic metaphor groups cohorts as tiles (by region, customer segm...
text: Design and prototype a new 'Traffic Mosaic' composer in the Redwood Console that lets engineers visually construct gradual canary and A/B traffic plans, preview live signal risk, and surfaces contextual rollback hints tied to telemetry thresholds. The mosaic metaphor groups cohorts as tiles (by region, customer segment, or traffic signature) with inline controls for % allocation, schedule, and guardrail attachments. Key goals: reduce cognitive load when building multi-stage rollouts, make rollback options discoverable, and enable quick simulation of traffic paths under different latency/error profiles.

title: Elastic breakpoints and overflow handling for dense Console surfaces (dashboards, rollouts, cost views)
preview: Objective: Implement an 'elastic breakpoints' system and overflow-handling patterns that keep data-dense Console pages (dashboards, rollouts, cost views) readable and interactive across viewport sizes and browsers. This ticket covers design decisions, prototypes, acceptance criteria, and cross-browser compatibility ...
text: Objective: Implement an 'elastic breakpoints' system and overflow-handling patterns that keep data-dense Console pages (dashboards, rollouts, cost views) readable and interactive across viewport sizes and browsers. This ticket covers design decisions, prototypes, acceptance criteria, and cross-browser compatibility notes for handoff to engineering. Motivation: We are seeing clipping, awkward wrapping, and scroll-jank on smaller viewports and on Safari/Firefox when multiple dense components (tables + charts + modals) appear together. The goal is a coherent set of layout rules and component-level fallbacks that prioritize information hierarchy, keyboard navigation, and low-latency reflow without heavy JS.

title: Embedded Canary Context Cards - Prototype Feedback & Accessibility Redlines
preview: Design and ship a set of small, embedded context cards for canary / canary-like rollouts in the Redwood Console. These cards surface experiment metadata (traffic split, evaluation metric summary, recent regressions), quick actions (pause, snapshot, annotate), and a lightweight CTA hierarchy intended for in-line deci...
text: Design and ship a set of small, embedded context cards for canary / canary-like rollouts in the Redwood Console. These cards surface experiment metadata (traffic split, evaluation metric summary, recent regressions), quick actions (pause, snapshot, annotate), and a lightweight CTA hierarchy intended for in-line decisioning without opening a full rollout inspector. This ticket captures the current prototype, high-level design decisions, redlines from accessibility review, and a prioritized QA/handoff checklist for engineering and PM.

title: Foldable Cost Attribution Tiles — prototype validation, redlines, and stakeholder playback
preview: Summary: This ticket captures the design review cycle for the new foldable (accordion-style) cost attribution tiles meant for the Console overview and rollout detail pages. The goal is to provide a compact, scannable cost summary that expands to reveal attribution slices, cost drivers, and quick-actions (drilldown, ...
text:
Summary: This ticket captures the design review cycle for the new foldable (accordion-style) cost attribution tiles meant for the Console overview and rollout detail pages. The goal is to provide a compact, scannable cost summary that expands to reveal attribution slices, cost drivers, and quick-actions (drilldown, compare, pin).

What we prototyped: A lightweight tile with three microstates — collapsed, summary-expanded (inline list + miniature sparkline), and full-expanded (detailed attribution graph + filter controls). Prototype: Figma link (internal): https://redwood.design/figma/foldable-cost-tiles (private). Playback recording: https://drive.redwood.com/recordings/cost-foldables-2026-02-26.mp4.

Stakeholder feedback (synthesis):
- PM (Aisha Rahman): Loves compact density but wants a clear signal when data is sampled vs. exact. Request: add a badge for "estimated" vs "exact" for ...

title: Stacked header collapse and inline density toggle for responsive virtual table
preview: Context: As we expand the virtualized table component to support denser dashboards and narrower layouts, product teams keep requesting: (1) a robust way to display multi-level column headers without consuming vertical real estate, and (2) an easy inline control for users to switch density without visiting a separate...
text: Context: As we expand the virtualized table component to support denser dashboards and narrower layouts, product teams keep requesting: (1) a robust way to display multi-level column headers without consuming vertical real estate, and (2) an easy inline control for users to switch density without visiting a separate settings panel. This ticket proposes: a stacked header pattern that can collapse into a single-line header per column on demand, paired with an inline density toggle (Compact / Comfortable / Readable) that persists per-user and integrates with our design tokens. Goals: reduce header height for dense views, maintain discoverability of grouped columns, keep keyboard and screen-reader accessibility, and avoid regressions in virtualization performance.

title: Reproducible customer-environment load harness for Private/VPC/on-prem
preview: Build a deterministic, customer-environment-aware load and replay harness for Private/VPC/on-prem deployments that produces reproducible tokens/sec and latency profiles (p50/p95/p99) and supports automated regression detection. The harness must capture environment metadata (OS/kernel, CUDA/cuDNN, driver, container r...
text: Build a deterministic, customer-environment-aware load and replay harness for Private/VPC/on-prem deployments that produces reproducible tokens/sec and latency profiles (p50/p95/p99) and supports automated regression detection. The harness must capture environment metadata (OS/kernel, CUDA/cuDNN, driver, container runtime, CPU topology), record and replay request traces with KV-cache warmup behavior, support synthetic traffic shaping, and integrate with Prometheus/Grafana and Redwood Console for per-run comparisons.

title: Perturbation-driven quant impact orchestrator and threshold auto-calibrator
preview: Goal: Build an orchestrator that runs targeted perturbation experiments whenever a quantization profile, model variant (AWQ/GPTQ), or runtime config changes and then auto-calibrates per-route thresholds used by the rollout gate. We want to detect semantic quality regressions for int8/int4 modes and recommend automat...
text:
Goal: Build an orchestrator that runs targeted perturbation experiments whenever a quantization profile, model variant (AWQ/GPTQ), or runtime config changes and then auto-calibrates per-route thresholds used by the rollout gate. We want to detect semantic quality regressions for int8/int4 modes and recommend automatic or manual rollbacks. This should be safe to run on CI for PRs and as a lightweight smoke suite in staging and can be scheduled for periodic drift scans in production cohorts.

Scope: 1) A perturbation job generator that synthesizes small, semantically-relevant perturbations (paraphrase substitutions, token deletions, numeric edge cases) per route and interrogates the quantized model variant. 2) A scoring pipeline that computes a normalized quality delta across metrics (exact-match when applicable, embedding cosine on reference responses, reranker-based rank-drop, and pro...

title: Normalize DST and plan-change hourly usage attribution to prevent negative invoice adjustments
preview:
Background:
Customers in EMEA/APAC saw sporadic negative invoice line items and duplicate credit adjustments for hourly metered plans after DST transitions or when a customer changed plan tiers mid-billing-cycle. Root cause appears to be a mismatch between: 1) how the hourly-aggregation batcher buckets events (UTC v...
text:
Background:
Customers in EMEA/APAC saw sporadic negative invoice line items and duplicate credit adjustments for hourly metered plans after DST transitions or when a customer changed plan tiers mid-billing-cycle. Root cause appears to be a mismatch between: 1) how the hourly-aggregation batcher buckets events (UTC vs tenant-local timezone offsets during DST transitions), 2) the proration logic that splits usage across plan versions, and 3) the NetSuite/Stripe export deduplication which assumes stable invoice boundary alignment.

Goals / Acceptance Criteria:
- Hourly usage aggregation buckets align deterministically for customers in DST-observing timezones so that a DST forward/backward shift does not create overlapping/empty hour buckets.
- When a customer changes plan mid-cycle, prorated usage attribution must be split by effective-plan-interval boundaries derived from the customer's...

title: Heartbeat admission lease failover to rescue dispatcher stalls and long-context tail
preview: Summary: We received three customer escalations (Hosted: low, Dedicated: high, Private: high) describing periodic p99+/tail latency spikes correlated with long-context sequences. Investigation pointed to a rare dispatcher stall where admission leases for short/latency-sensitive requests were not being renewed under ...
text: Summary: We received three customer escalations (Hosted: low, Dedicated: high, Private: high) describing periodic p99+/tail latency spikes correlated with long-context sequences. Investigation pointed to a rare dispatcher stall where admission leases for short/latency-sensitive requests were not being renewed under mixed-length waves, causing queue starvation for small requests and batch amplification for long requests. Root cause hypothesis: an instance-level admission lease state was allowed to lapse when the heartbeat renewal path raced with a KV cache compaction event and an internal prefetch quarantine, producing a transient token that left the admission window closed until the next scheduler tick.

title: Policy exemption transitive fanout causing unmetered requests
preview: Summary: We observed a class of requests that bypass metering when a per-route exemption includes an alias/wildcard and the request fanouts to multiple internal sub-routes. The policy evaluator memoizes exemption results keyed insufficiently, causing exemptions to be applied transitively to child routes. This result...
text: Summary: We observed a class of requests that bypass metering when a per-route exemption includes an alias/wildcard and the request fanouts to multiple internal sub-routes. The policy evaluator memoizes exemption results keyed insufficiently, causing exemptions to be applied transitively to child routes. This results in missing meter events and underbilling for affected orgs during fanout-heavy workloads.

title: VPC/On-Prem Upgrade Safeguard Coordinator — LTS rollout & orchestrated migrations
preview: Goal: Build a resilient upgrade coordinator for Redwood Private (VPC + on-prem) that manages LTS channels, executes orchestrated migrations, and enforces rollout gates and rollback semantics. The coordinator will be an operator-style control plane component (controller+workers) that consumes an upgrade plan, perform...
text:
Goal: Build a resilient upgrade coordinator for Redwood Private (VPC + on-prem) that manages LTS channels, executes orchestrated migrations, and enforces rollout gates and rollback semantics. The coordinator will be an operator-style control plane component (controller+workers) that consumes an upgrade plan, performs preflight checks, schedules batched canaries, runs migrations (schema, filesystem, config), verifies postflight health, and either advances the rollout or triggers an automated rollback.

Scope:
- LTS channel awareness: mapping of releases to LTS channels and migration policies (forced vs opt-in).
- Upgrade plan model: DAG of steps (preflight -> canary cohorts -> data migrations -> global rollout) with idempotency and durable checkpoints.
- Orchestrated migrations: support for DB schema migrations (transactional where possible), KV cache migrations, and operator CRD versi...
